questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements
questions.q_txt: It is the process of predicting the future weather conditions for a certain location.
questions.a_txt: Weather forecasting.

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements
questions.q_txt: Weather station shelter.
questions.a_txt: Stevenson screen.

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements
questions.q_txt: The technology used in oceans to collect weather data.
questions.a_txt: Weather buoys.

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements
questions.q_txt: The technology for collecting weather data that is launched into the air carried by a balloon.
questions.a_txt: Radiosonde (radio probe).

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements
questions.q_txt: Stations for collecting and recording weather data.
questions.a_txt: Weather stations.

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements
questions.q_txt: A technique that orbit the Earth to collect weather data.
questions.a_txt: Satellites.

questions.ex_title: Write the scientific term of each of the following statements
questions.q_txt: Storms are loaded with sand and dust blow over Egypt in the spring season.
questions.a_txt: Khamaseen winds.

questions.q_txt: Weather forecasting is achieved by collecting, recording, and analyzing data related to temperature and _____ speed and the _____ tracks.
Weather _____ is used in oceans and balloons carrying _____ in the air, as modern techniques for collecting data about the weather.
The radiosonde is launched twice a day, once at _____ and once at _____.
The _____ sensors transmit weather data to ground stations via _____ waves.
The sensors in the radiosonde used in the oceans send the data to observation centers.
The structure of the Stevenson screen is painted green.
The radiosonde is known as the electronic probe.
Balloons carrying the radiosonde are launched once every day.
The balloon carrying the radiosonde bursts due to decreasing of humidity.
Weather forecasting is absolute facts.
Khamaseen winds blow in the autumn season.

questions.ex_title: Mark (T) or (X) for each statement, with correction
questions.q_txt: Satellites are used in weather forecasts.

questions.ex_title: Mark (T) or (X) for each statement, with correction
questions.q_txt: Storm tracks can be predicted by satellites.

questions.ex_title: Mark (T) or (X) for each statement, with correction
questions.q_txt: The balloon carrying the radiosonde is filled with oxygen gas.

questions.ex_title: Mark (T) or (X) for each statement, with correction
questions.q_txt: The size of the balloon carrying the radiosonde decreases as it rises upward.

questions.ex_title: Mark (T) or (X) for each statement, with correction
questions.q_txt: The radiosonde falls by a parachute after the balloon carrying it bursts.

questions.ex_title: Mark (T) or (X) for each statement, with correction
questions.q_txt: The summer season in Egypt has weather disturbance that lead to the formation of sandstorms.
Among the weather forecasting on the ground techniques are _____
The Stevenson screen is _____
The radiosonde carries instruments to measure all of the following, except _____
Which of the following describes the radiosonde?
mcq_choices: Rises upward by an air buoy.

questions.q_txt: The data received by the radiosonde sensors is transmitted via _____
All of the following are fields of benefit from weather forecasts, except _____
Painting the wood of the Stevenson screen white.
questions.a_txt: To reflect the sunlight.

questions.q_txt: The presence of slanted openings in the weather station shelter.
questions.a_txt: To allow the entry of air.

questions.q_txt: Placing Stevenson screen above the ground level.
questions.a_txt: To protect the measuring instruments inside it from the heat radiated by the Earth's surface.

questions.q_txt: Placing the Stevenson screen away from trees and buildings.
questions.a_txt: To avoid any obstacles that may affect the measurements.

questions.q_txt: The radiosonde balloon is filled with helium gas or hydrogen gas.
questions.a_txt: Because they are among the lightest gases due to their low densities.

questions.q_txt: The radiosonde is known as the radio probe.
questions.a_txt: Because it sends the data received by the radiosonde sensors via radio waves to ground stations.

questions.q_txt: The radiosonde balloon bursts after it is launched into the air.
questions.a_txt: As a result of its continuous expansion due to the decrease in atmospheric pressure as altitude increases.

questions.q_txt: Weather forecasting is not absolute facts, but rather probabilistic predictions.
questions.a_txt: Because the atmosphere is affected by a large number of variables.

questions.q_txt: Khamaseen winds negatively affect the health of the respiratory system.
questions.a_txt: Because these winds are loaded with sand and dust.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance of each of the following
questions.q_txt: Stevenson screen (weather station shelter).
questions.a_txt: Measuring temperature, atmospheric pressure and humidity in the place where it is placed.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance of each of the following
questions.q_txt: Weather buoys.
questions.a_txt: Collecting the data received in the oceans from the sensors to organize and process it, then sending it via satellites to observation centers around the world.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance of each of the following
questions.q_txt: Helium gas (or hydrogen gas) for the radiosonde balloon.
questions.a_txt: Carrying radiosonde balloons upward.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance of each of the following
questions.q_txt: Radio waves for the radiosonde.
questions.a_txt: Sending the data received by the radiosonde sensors to ground stations.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance of each of the following
questions.q_txt: The parachute for the radiosonde.
questions.a_txt: Landing of the radiosonde device to Earth after the balloon bursts.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance of each of the following
questions.q_txt: Weather forecasts in the protection of lives and property.
questions.a_txt: Taking appropriate preventive measures when there is a probability of the occurrence of hot or cold waves, or the occurrence of floods or thunderstorms.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance of each of the following
questions.q_txt: Weather forecasts in supporting economic activities.
questions.a_txt: Helping farmers determine the timing of planting, irrigation, harvesting crops and pest control, which increases agricultural productivity and reduces losses.

questions.ex_title: Mention the importance of each of the following
questions.q_txt: Weather forecasts in aviation safety.
questions.a_txt: Avoiding weather conditions that may threaten air navigation traffic.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer the questions
questions.q_txt: The opposite figure shows a single balloon capable of flying upward, at sea level and at a mountain peak: How do you explain the difference in the size of the balloon in the two cases? (A diagram shows a red balloon at the base of a mountain and a larger red balloon at the peak).
questions.a_txt: Due to the difference in atmospheric pressure, the atmospheric pressure at the mountain peak is lower than the atmospheric pressure at the mountain base.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer the questions
questions.q_txt: The opposite figure shows a single balloon capable of flying upward, at sea level and at a mountain peak: What are the two gases that can be used to fill the balloon to make it capable of flying upward? With explanation.
questions.a_txt: Hydrogen and helium gases, because they are among the lightest gases due to their low densities.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer the questions
questions.q_txt: The opposite figure shows one of the techniques used in weather forecasting: What is the name of this technique? (A diagram shows a blue wooden box with slanted openings on a stand).
questions.a_txt: Stevenson screen (weather station shelter).

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer the questions
questions.q_txt: The opposite figure shows one of the techniques used in weather forecasting: What is the material from which this box is made, and what colour is used to paint it? With explanation.
questions.a_txt: Wood painted white, to reflect the sunlight.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer the questions
questions.q_txt: The opposite figure shows one of the techniques used in weather forecasting: What is the value of the height X? And why it is placed at this height from ground level.
questions.a_txt: 120 cm, to protect the measuring instruments inside it from the heat radiated by the Earth's surface.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer the questions
questions.q_txt: The opposite figure shows one of the techniques used in weather forecasting: What are the devices that placed inside the box?
questions.a_txt: Devices that measure temperature, atmospheric pressure and humidity.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer the questions
questions.q_txt: The opposite figure shows one of the weather forecasting techniques (3) that is carried by the part (1) until it bursts at a certain altitude from the Earth's surface and lands by the tool (2): Replace the data (1), (2), (3) with what is appropriate. (A diagram shows a balloon (1) connected to a parachute (2) which is connected to a device box (3)).
questions.a_txt: (1): Balloon. (2): Parachute. (3): Device of radiosonde.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer the questions
questions.q_txt: The opposite figure shows one of the weather forecasting techniques (3) that is carried by the part (1) until it bursts at a certain altitude from the Earth's surface and lands by the tool (2): Why is part (1) filled with helium gas?
questions.a_txt: Because it is a light gas due to its low density.

questions.ex_title: Study the following figures, then answer the questions
questions.q_txt: The opposite figure shows one of the weather forecasting techniques (3) that is carried by the part (1) until it bursts at a certain altitude from the Earth's surface and lands by the tool (2): How do the sensors of part (3) send the data to the ground stations?
questions.a_txt: Sends it via radio waves.

questions.q_txt: What is meant by weather forecasting?
questions.a_txt: It is the process of predicting the future weather conditions for a certain location.

questions.q_txt: Compare between weather buoys and radiosonde «In terms of: Their location - Weather data sent».
questions.a_txt: Weather buoys: Location: They float on the surface of oceans water. Weather data sent: The data received from the sensors is sent, after being organized and processed, to observation centers around the world via satellites. Radiosonde: Location: It is released into the air carried by a balloon filled with hydrogen or helium gas. Weather data sent: The data received from the sensors is sent via radio waves to ground stations.

questions.q_txt: Explain the cause and effect in the relationship between the size of the radiosonde balloon and atmospheric pressure.
questions.a_txt: Cause: atmospheric pressure decreases with altitude above sea level. Effect: the size of the radiosonde balloon increases until it bursts.

questions.q_txt: Explain the cause and effect in the relationship between Khamaseen winds and respiratory system health.
questions.a_txt: Cause: the blowing of Khamaseen winds loaded with dust and sand in the spring season. Effect: negatively affects the health of the respiratory system.

questions.q_txt: Mention the techniques used in collecting probabilistic weather data.
questions.a_txt: Stevenson screen (weather station shelter), Weather buoys, Radiosonde (radio probe), Satellites, Weather stations.

questions.q_txt: What is the other name given to each of the following: (1) Weather station shelter. (2) Radiosonde.
questions.a_txt: (1) Stevenson screen. (2) Radio probe.

questions.q_txt: The Stevenson screen is considered one of the weather forecasting techniques: (1) What is the height of the shelter box? And what is the height of the box above the ground level?
questions.a_txt: Height of the shelter box: 65 cm. Height of the box above the ground level: 120 cm.

questions.q_txt: The Stevenson screen is considered one of the weather forecasting techniques: (2) Mention one similarity and one difference between the Stevenson screen and the radiosonde.
questions.a_txt: Similarity: both measure temperature, atmospheric pressure and humidity. Difference: the Stevenson screen is fixed near the ground level, while the radiosonde is launched into the air.

questions.q_txt: Give a brief summary about each of the following: (1) Weather buoys.
questions.a_txt: They are used in the oceans, collecting the data received from the sensors to organize and process it, then send it via satellites to observation centers around the world.

questions.q_txt: Give a brief summary about each of the following: (2) Weather stations.
questions.a_txt: A technique used for weather forecasting, distributed in different regions in all countries of the world.

questions.q_txt: The radiosonde is one of the techniques for collecting weather forecasting data: (1) How does the radiosonde ascend? And how does it falls?
questions.a_txt: The radiosonde ascends by balloons filled with hydrogen or helium gas and descends after the balloon bursts by parachute.

questions.q_txt: The radiosonde is one of the techniques for collecting weather forecasting data: (2) When does the radiosonde launch during the day?
questions.a_txt: Twice, once at noon and the other at midnight.

questions.q_txt: The radiosonde is one of the techniques for collecting weather forecasting data: (3) Why does the radiosonde balloon burst at an altitude of about 30 km?
questions.a_txt: Because of its continuous expansion due to the decrease in atmospheric pressure with altitude above sea level.

questions.q_txt: Explain the importance of following weather forecasts during weather instability. «Mention only two points».
questions.a_txt: Wearing suitable clothing during weather fluctuations. Avoiding crowded places. Making the right decision about travel by land or sea.

questions.ex_title: Higher-order Questions: Choose the correct answer
questions.q_txt: From the opposite diagram: Which of the following arrows shows the Coriolis Effect on wind direction? (A diagram shows Earth with latitude lines and arrows A, B, C, D representing wind directions).
